interactions.summary: Jiang closes by saying Marx would be disgusted by a world where humans enslave themselves as consumers; Freud and psychology explain the shift from community to the self.

claims.claim: Jiang says Marx would be horrified by the shared outcome of communism and capitalism: humans reduced to consumers whose life script is school, money, and buying things.
The next stage of the course will explain modern self-enslavement through Freud: psychology shifts attention from world and community to the individual self.
claims.temporal_scope: Course transition from Marx to Freud.

claims.claim: Jiang's final moral is that freedom and happiness are collective: if everyone is unhappy, no individual can be happy by private self-focus alone.
claims.temporal_scope: Closing thesis of the lecture dated 2025-05-29.

signature_moments.moment: Humanity has chosen to enslave itself for no good reason.
signature_moments.source_phrase: humanity has chosen to enslave themselves
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the episode a brutal ending: Marx was wrong about history but right to hate the consumer prison.

signature_moments.moment: You cannot be free and happy alone if everyone else is unhappy.
signature_moments.source_phrase: only if people are free and happy together
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It compresses the final bridge from Marx/Bakunin to Freud and modern selfhood.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's description of modern self-enslavement to schooling, money-making, and buying things.
The psychological turn from collective consciousness and community toward the belief that individual happiness can be pursued alone.

chronology_notes.note: The lecture ends by explicitly setting up Freud as the next class after Marx.

uncertainty_notes.note: Transcript says 'Simeon Freud'; context indicates Sigmund Freud.
